**Title: Scooby-Doo and the Great Volleyball Van-Venture!**

***[The episode opens with Scooby, Shaggy, and Scrappy hurtling down a road in shambles, their van careening wildly as they head toward the finishing school.]***

Shaggy: "Like, Scoob, are you sure we're going the right way to Miss Grimwood's Finishing School for Ghouls? My map just has a doodle of a sandwich here."

Scooby (steering badly): "Ruh-roh! Me no know!"

Scrappy: "Uncles, fear not! With my superhero powers, not finding the school would be... actually, pretty embarrassing, never mind."

***[They eventually crash spectacularly into something that suspiciously looks like a volleyball net.]***

Fred (from afar, shouting): "That's another point for proving law disobeying, ladies and gents!"

Shaggy: "Like, hey! Nobody invited you, Fred!"

***[The gang stumbles out of the van and straight into Miss Grimwood's welcoming arms.]***

Miss Grimwood: "Ah, you must be the new gym teachers!"

Shaggy: "New gym teachers? More like bemused slam-ditchers, with all due respect, ma'am."

Scooby (snickering): "Ri-mused clam-switchers!"

***[Cut to the gang and Grimwood’s ghoulish girls in the gym, discussing strategy for the next volleyball game.]***

Shaggy: "So... Winnie Werewolf, slobbering over the volleyball isn’t, like, allowed, right?"

Winnie (panting): "Got it! No slobber!"

Scrappy (with a superhero pose): "Fear not, for Scrappy-Doo is here to coach you in the ways of supernatural spiking!"

***[Shaggy and Scooby are left to study their opponents: a team coached by none other than Revolta, the spider-witch.]***

Shaggy: "So Revolta wants to snag you girls for... what? A volleyball league of evil?"

Scooby: "Ruh-roh! That's a baaaaad web!"

***[Throughout training, the gang makes a myriad jokes about everyone and everything around.]***

Shaggy: "And look at Grim Creeper, Revolta's assistant. Like, what's the deal? If I wanted gardening tips, I'd ask my fern, not a walking talk-show for plants."

Scooby (laughing): "Rurn the other cheek, Grim Creeper!"

***[During the climactic volleyball game, Revolta attempts to cast a spell.]***

Revolta: "Now, my pretties, you will—"

Scrappy (interrupting): "What's with the 'my pretties'? What are we, in a budget production of the Wizard of Oz?"

***[Shaggy and Scooby concoct a plan involving disguising themselves as Revolta and Grim Creeper to spook them off. The plan, ripe with mistaken identities, ultimately falls apart hilariously.]***

Shaggy (as Revolta): "I demand you stop this... wait, like, why am I talking to myself?"

***[In the end, Scrappy uses his superhero powers to save the day, while Shaggy and Scooby accidentally land the winning point against Revolta’s team.]***

Shaggy: "Like, who knew all it took to win was conjuring more chaos than a last-call sale at a tie-dye shop."

Scooby: "Rhehehehe!"

***[As they pile back into the van, Fred attempts to hitch a ride.]***

Fred: "Hey guys, how about a lift?"

Shaggy: "Only if you promise to not crash it to prove any 'points', Fred."

Fred: "No promises."

***[The van drives off into the sunset, the sound of their laughter and off-key singing mingling with Fred’s rants about traffic laws.]***

**[Cut to Red Herring, tied up in volleyball nets, glaring.]**

Red Herring: "One day, Fred... one day."

***[Freeze-frame as they all laugh at a joke Scooby just made, fade to black.]***

***THE END***

**Title: Scooby-Doo and the Curious Case of Deja Vu**

**[The episode opens with Scooby, Shaggy, and the ever-heroic Scrappy driving Shaggy's Van, seemingly lost yet again.]**

Shaggy: "Man, Scoob, why does this feel like we've done this before?"

Scooby: "Reah, rike déjà vu!"

Scrappy: "Don't worry! Scrappy-Doo will lead us to victory... again!"

**[They arrive at Miss Grimwood's, but everything's slightly off. The names aren't quite right, and the gang immediately notices.]**

Shaggy: "Wait a sec, wasn't it Miss Grimwood’s School for Girls? What’s with the 'Similar View Only' banner?"

Miss Grimwood ("Performance Only"): "Welcome, visitors! I'm Miss Grimwood, as always."

Scooby (whispering to Shaggy): "Rerformance only?"

**[Each character introduction is riddled with bizarre title changes. The gang can't help but comment on every single one.]**

Teddy Wolf ("Performance Only"): "I'm <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON> now, not Winnie Werewolf. A name change was due. Branding, you know."

Scrappy: "Teddy? But you don't look like a teddy bear. What's next, 'Sybil' instead of Sibella?"

Sybil ("Foreign Only"): : "Precisely, darling Scrappy. It's all in the rebrand."

**[As they meet each character, the 'similar', 'view only', 'performance only' tags create a comedic confusion. The team questions whether they're in an alternate universe or a poorly executed rebrand. Even the villains get in on the action.]**

Revolta ("View Only"): "Ah, my dear opponents. I've gone through a bit of an image overhaul. Now, I’m 'Revolution', view only, of course."

Grim Creeper ("Intro Only"): "And I'm merely an intro now. Boo."

Shaggy: "Like, since when did villains care about personal branding?"

**[Their investigation leads them from one changed character to another, each more bizarrely labeled than the last.]**

Mirror Monster ("Visible Only"): "Behold, for I am now merely a visual effect!"

Shaggy: "Dude, are we chasing copyrights now?"

**[Even the non-speaking entities have updated statuses, adding to the layers of absurdity.]**

Octopus Butler ("Solo Edit"): "..."
  
Scooby: "He's 'solo' now but still can't speak. Rinteresting!"

**[Throughout the episode, they can't shake the feeling of déjà vu. Their clever ad-libbing about the changes only heightens the humor.]**

Scrappy (heroically): "Fear not! Scrappy-Doo will solve the mystery of the rebrand... or at least figure out who's behind all these 'subscription only' features!"

**[Eventually, they confront "Revolution", her plans foiled once again, despite her new 'brand'.]**

Revolta ("Shocked Face"): "Curses! Foiled by Scrappy-Doo and his insistence on continuity!"

**[In the end, they pile back into Shaggy's Van, their adventure concluded but their confusion about the world's inconsistencies remains. Yet, their spirits remain high, laughing off the absurdities.]**

Shaggy: "Like, do you think next time we'll be 'view only', Scoob?"

Scooby (chuckling): "Rehehe, rmaybe!"

**[As they drive off, the episode closes on a note of camaraderie and hilarity, the trio ready to face whatever strange rebrand the world throws at them next, together.]**

**THE END**

**Title: Scooby-Doo and the Mystery of the Mixed-Up Monikers**

**[The scene opens with Scooby, Shaggy, and 'He Looked Worried', formerly known as Scrappy-Doo, cruising in the Shaggy Van from "Scooby-Doo and the 13 Ghosts". The group seems particularly pensive.]**

Shaggy: "Man, Scoob, do you feel like things have been a little... off lately?"

Scooby (nodding): "Ruh-huh, everything's reranged!"

He Looked Worried: "And why does everyone call me 'He Looked Worried' now? I'm Scrappy-Doo, darn it!"

**[They arrive at the familiar yet differently labeled Miss Greenwood's, where they're greeted by a cast of characters all sporting bizarre new names or descriptors.]**

Cool (pointing at himself vacantly): "..."

Shaggy: "Zoinks! Even Cool doesn't have lines anymore. What's going on here?"

**[As they encounter each character, their confusion grows.]**

Winnie the Pooh (just watching): "..."

He Looked Worried: "Shouldn't you be in the Hundred Acre Wood instead of a haunted finishing school?"

**[Every character interaction includes awkward pauses or confused ad-libs as our heroes try to make sense of the nonsensical titles.]**

Cancel (just existing): "..."

Shaggy: "Wait, who's 'Cancel'? And why does that sound like a command instead of a name?"

Scooby: "And what about rife is wonderful? Is that a villain or an inspirational poster?"

**[Even the creatures and entities are not spared from this odd rebranding.]**

Octopus Butler (flailing nonsensically): "..."

He Looked Worried: "I miss when he was just a regular octopus butler with no boxing ambitions."

**[The trio makes it their mission to uncover the reason behind this bizarre world of altered identities.]**

Scooby: "Re need to rigure this rout!"

**[Each clue leads them in circles, their reality seeming more like a badly strung-together TV show pilot than their usual escapades.]**

Crystal Monster (just standing there, being oddly non-threatening): "..."

Shaggy: "Like, since when did monsters start respecting personal space?"

**[Amid their investigation, they discover a character behind the scenes, 'The Great Renamer', responsible for all the nonsensical changes.]**

He Looked Worried, assertively: "We demand you change everything back! Especially my name!"

The Great Renamer (cackling): "But 'He Looked Worried' has such a nice ring to it!"

**[With clever teamwork and a bit of classic Scooby-Doo shenanigans involving a wild chase scene, they manage to corner The Great Renamer.]**

The Great Renamer (defeated): "Alright, alright! Your names and everything will go back to normal!"

**[As everything snaps back to its original form, the gang, now properly named again, breathes a sigh of relief.]**

Shaggy: "Like, it's good to have everything back to normal, right Scoob?"

Scooby: "Reah! Rincluding my rame!"

**[The episode ends with them driving off into their next adventure, the Shaggy Van bouncing along the road back to normalcy, or as normal as things ever are for our intrepid trio.]**

He Looked Worried (joyfully): "Scrappy-Doo power to the rescue! I mean... just being a part of the team is cool too."

**[Freeze frame on their laughter, with a 'The End' sign swinging comedically into the scene.]**

**THE END**

**Title: Scooby-Doo and the Case of the Constant Changes**

**[The episode opens with Scooby, Shaggy, and Scrappy driving through a fog-swathed road, each visibly puzzled.]**

Shaggy: "Guys, does anyone else feel like we're living the same day over, but everything's just slightly... off?"

Scrappy: "You mean like how I'm suddenly 'Scrappy do' and not 'Scrappy-Doo'?"

Scooby: "Ryeah! And why is everything 'view only' or 'appearance only'? It's like we're in a weird TV guide!"

**[They arrive at what should be Grimwood's School, but now it’s listed as 'Performance (see only) (Strange)'.]**

Mr. Grimwood: "..."

Shaggy: "Hey, Mr. Grimwood, why the silent treatment? And what's with the '(Appearance only)'?"

**[The gang encounters each character, each one oddly absent of their usual characteristics.]**

Winnie the Poop (nodding silently towards them): "..."

Scrappy: "Okay, 'Winnie the Poop'? Really? What's next, 'Sybil' only for external use?"

Sybil (standing stoically, not engaging): "..."

**[They learn of a new villain named 'Chaos', linked with the strange alterations occurring.]**

Shaggy: "So, this 'Chaos' character is just 'View only'? How do we 'view' our way out of this one?"

**[The gang splits up to gather clues, each encountering various oddly labeled characters and objects.]**

Keyboard (lying inconspicuously on the ground): "..."

Scooby: "Rook! A 'Keyboard (external only)'! What does that even mean?"

**[Discovering a pattern in the changes, they deduce that 'Chaos' is altering reality itself, making their world behave like a cross between a TV show and a museum exhibition.]**

Scrappy: "So, let me get this straight. 'Chaos' turns our world into a 'screen only' exhibit? Time to scrappy-do away with this chaos!"

**[The trio decides to confront 'Chaos', but first, they must navigate a series of bizarre obstacles, including the 'Two-headed shark (pictured only)' and the 'Flying Venus Trap (Scene Only)'.]**

Two-headed shark (frozen image in their path): "..."

Shaggy: "Man, even the monsters are 'pictured only'. How do we get past something we can’t touch?"

**[Utilizing their ingenuity (and a bit of cartoon logic), they manage to 'view' their way past hazards. Eventually, they corner 'Chaos' in a place marked 'The Final Scene'.]**

Chaos: "..."

Shaggy (confronting Chaos): "Alright, 'Chaos', it's time to put things back to how they were. We want our reality back, not this 'view only' world!"

**[With a dramatic flourish, Scrappy manages to snatch a remote-like device from 'Chaos', hitting a big red button marked 'Reset'.]**

**[Everything flashes brightly, and suddenly the world snaps back to its original setting, each character and object returning to their familiar state.]**

Scooby: "Rit worked! Reverything's back to rormal!"

**[Their adventure concludes with the trio reflecting on their bizarre journey as they drive off in the Shaggy Van, heading towards their next mystery.]**

Shaggy: "You know, I think I preferred it when the monsters were real, and not 'view only'."

Scrappy: "And I’m just glad to be 'Scrappy-Doo' again. 'Scrappy do' just didn’t have the same ring to it."

Scooby: "Ryeah! Scooby-Dooby-Doo!"

**[The screen freezes on their laughter, closing the episode with the mystery solved and reality restored.]**

**THE END**

**Title: Scooby-Doo and the Riddle of the Renamed Reality**

**[The episode opens with Scooby, now referred to simply as 'Scooby Doo,' Shaggy, who has confusingly been dubbed 'Pink Roger,' and Scrappy, known as 'Who Likes to Fight,' cruising down a foggy path in the Shaggy Van, an eerie sense of déjà vu hanging in the air.]**

Pink Roger: "Like, does anyone else remember when I was Shaggy, and colors weren't part of our names?"

Scooby Doo: "Ryeah, and rwhy is Scrappy 'Who likes to fight'? Rhat's kinda on-the-nose, don't ya think?"

Who Likes to Fight: "I do tend to jump into the fray, but let's focus on unraveling this mystery, folks!"

**[Their journey leads them to an all-too-familiar school, now marked as 'OK (picture only)'. Each character they encounter introduces themselves with a bizarrely modified moniker or a strangely specific label.]**

Lady. Grimwood: "Welcome to my home, travelers. I sense your confusion."

Pink Roger: "Yeah, 'cause your sign says 'one character,' Lady. Grimwood. What’s up with that?"

Wolf Bear: "Roooaar! I mean, hello. Yes, I'm a 'one character' too. It's quite limiting, honestly."

**[They notice that all characters, including villains and various other entities, are tagged with peculiar descriptors, 'external only,' 'one image,' and even 'offline.']**

Resistor (gesturing vaguely): "I’m your nemesis for this episode, but apparently, I'm 'external only,' so I can't physically interact. Quite frustrating."

Who Likes to Fight: "That’s convenient. So, how exactly do we 'fight' this battle?"

**[As they delve deeper into the mystery, the trio finds that their reality has been altered, turning everyone into mere shadows of their former selves—representations without substance.]**

Octopus Butler (pointedly still): "…"

Scooby Doo: "Rook! The Octopus Butler is 'Home (Offline).' Does that mean we can't even ask for snacks?"

**[Combining their wit, courage, and a knack for breaking the fourth wall, they track clues leading to 'People (image only),' a mysterious force altering their reality.]**

Pink Roger: "This is like being stuck in a really bad video game where everyone's just NPC's!"

**[Their investigation unveils that 'Mai Nkwam's Letter,' a cryptic reference hiding in plain sight, is key to solving their predicament. It reveals that their altered names and labels are part of a grand puzzle.]**

Who Likes to Fight (scratching his head): "Mai Nkwam...Wait! That's 'I am Known' backward! Someone's playing with us, changing our world into a riddle!"

**[Deciphering the puzzle, they confront 'People (image only),' revealing it to be a confused entity named 'ID Crisis,' responsible for the name changes and weird labels, trying to find its own identity by reshuffling theirs.]**

ID Crisis (apologetically): "I just wanted to know who I could be by changing who you all were. I didn't realize identities were so... personal."

**[With understanding and a bit of heart-to-heart conversation, they manage to convince ID Crisis to revert everything back to normal, restoring their original names and the world around them.]**

Pink Roger (smiling): "You know, 'ID Crisis,' finding out who you are takes time. You can't just steal or change others' identities to figure it out."

Scooby Doo: "Ryeah, everyone's runique in rtheir own ray!"

**[As they bid farewell to ID Crisis, now embarked on a journey of self-discovery, our trio drives off into the sunset, their reality restored but ever vigilant for the next bizarre adventure.]**

Who Likes to Fight: "Remember, if anyone messes with reality again, they’ll have to answer to 'Who Likes to Fight!' Uh, I mean Scrappy-Doo!"

**[The episode ends with a hearty laugh shared among the trio, zooming out from the Shaggy Van as it veers off onto the next mystery, their bonds stronger and their identities unquestionably their own once more.]**

**THE END**

**[We open on a spooky, moonlit night in Transylvania, with Scooby, Shaggy Werewolf, and Sudarika (a mysteriously renamed Scrappy-Doo), speeding towards the castle of Count Dracula for the infamous Dracula Monster Road Race.]**

**Scooby Doo**: "Rooby-Rooby-Roo, I don't remrember signing up for this!"

**Shaggy Werewolf**: "Like, man, being a werewolf is bad enough, but having to race against all of Dracula's monster pals too? That's just icing on the spooky cake."

**Sudarika did it (Scrappy)**: "Don't worry, Uncle Shaggy! With a little Scrappy power and some detective work, we'll get you back to your old self in no time!"

**[Googie, checking in with the gang, is more determined than ever to support Shaggy in this lupine debacle.]**

**Googie**: "Hang tight, Shag! We've got a plan. Who knew 'check it out, Shaggy's friend' would mean being your pit crew in a monster rally?"

**[As they arrive at the castle, the list of competitors reads like a who’s who of the monster world, each described in odd terms such as "one note," "for illustrative purposes only," and "example."]**

**Count Dracula (First Step)**: "Welcome, racers! To win back your humanity, Shaggy Werewolf, you must prove your mettle on my treacherous track."

**Wolfgang (one note)**: "Just a heads up, furball. This course has more twists than a pretzel."

**[With odd descriptors for the competitors like “Sloknis Monster (visible only)” and “Internet Boy (Example),” the gang can’t help but feel the surreal nature of their situation.]**

**Sudarika did it**: "Something's fishy here, and it's not the Crocodile (view only). These descriptions... It’s like we’re in a badly programmed video game!"

**[As the race commences, the gang uses their mystery-solving skills to unveil that Dracula’s race is a magical facade designed to trap unwitting competitors in a perpetual loop of challenges.]**

**Shaggy Werewolf**: "So, if we break the loop, I can be me again? Sounds like a mystery to solve while racing!"

**[Utilizing their iconic teamwork, the gang, with the help of the "view only" and "for display only" characters, manage to decipher the clues leading to breaking Dracula's magical cycle.]**

**Googie**: "Every race checkpoint has a puzzle piece. Solve it, and we solve this!"

**[In a thrilling finish, Shaggy Werewolf, with assistance from Scooby and Sudarika, crosses the finish line first, breaking the spell and returning Shaggy to his human form.]**

**Count Dracula**: "Curses! Foiled by friendship and cleverness! Very well, Shaggy Rogers, you are free from my curse."

**Shaggy Rogers**: "Like, it feels good to be back. And I couldn’t have done it without my best pals…and being a werewolf racer, I guess?"

**[The episode closes with the gang driving away from Transylvania, sharing laughs and looking forward to their next adventure, puzzled yet invigorated by the strange descriptors that made this race anything but ordinary.]**

**Sudarika did it**: "On to the next mystery! And hopefully, less confusing race categories!"

**Scooby Doo**: "Reah, less ronfusing, more Scooby Snacks!"

**[The scene fades out on their laughter, promising more zany adventures for our intrepid team.]**

**THE END**

new_dialogue.text: Quick question: if I give you a messy list of characters and weird labels, can you still turn it into a scene where everyone gets at least one line?

Yes—if you paste the list, I can: (1) treat every entry as a character (even objects/animals), (2) give each at least one spoken line, and (3) weave the labels (like “view only,” “image only”) into jokes or plot constraints. Do you want it as a script transcript with stage directions, or more like prose with dialogue?
Transcript style. Also, how do you handle entries that say “no lines”?

For a transcript where “everyone gets dialogue,” I’d reinterpret “no lines” as an in-world rule the characters complain about, then still give them a line anyway (e.g., they whisper, use a sign, or break the rule on purpose). Alternatively, I can keep them silent but give them “dialogue” via captions or another character quoting them—your call.
Captions could be funny. Give me an example with two monsters and a random object.

MONSTER A: "Why do I have a tag that says ‘VISIBLE ONLY’? I’m literally standing right here."
MONSTER B: "At least you’re visible. Mine says ‘EXTERNAL ONLY.’ I’m apparently not even allowed indoors."
RANDOM OBJECT (caption on-screen): ["I’M ‘DISPLAY ONLY’ AND YET SOMEONE KEEPS MOVING ME. THIS IS A WORKPLACE SAFETY VIOLATION."]
MONSTER A: "Did… did the furniture just file a complaint?"
MONSTER B: "Yep. And it’s more coherent than the plot."
That works. Last thing: can the scene keep calling out continuity errors without needing to recap everything?

Definitely. The characters can react in-the-moment—"Wait, wasn’t this hallway here yesterday?"—and treat the changes as obvious and annoying, like a glitch they’re stuck living through. If you want, I can also add a running gag where a ‘Continuity Officer’ (a clipboard-wielding background character) keeps insisting everything is normal.

Next episode: keep the same reality-glitch vibe, but crank it up to full-on *label soup* again—like, if somebody can be tagged “external only,” I want the gang roasting that tag for ten straight seconds. And if the van’s name morphs even slightly, they should treat it like a personal insult.

Got it—more aggressive tag-mocking and a running bit where any tiny van-name change becomes a full-blown grievance.

**Title: Scooby-Doo and the Tag-Soup Tantrum**

**[Cold open: The van pulls up to a cliffside overlook that absolutely should not have a parking spot. A fresh decal on the door reads: “Shaggy Vanne (typo only).”]**

Scooby: “Ruh-roh… Raggy… it’s not even spelled right. ‘Vanne’??”

Shaggy: “Like, that’s not a typo, Scoob—that’s a *threat.* Last week it was ‘Shaggy Wayne,’ before that ‘Shaggy Wan.’ Who’s doing drive-by rebrands on our vehicle?”

Scrappy: “Let me at ‘em! Nobody disrespects our ride with a silent E! I’ll fight a dictionary!”

**[A banner flaps overhead: “WELCOME: (VIEW ONLY) (NO TOUCHING) (FOR DISPLAY PURPOSES).”]**

Shaggy: “Oh cool, a whole location that’s ‘view only.’ So we’re sightseeing in a mystery now?”

Scooby: “Ryeah—like a museum where the exhibits chase you!”

**[A guard steps out. His name tag literally reads: “Guard (appearance only) (still talking anyway).”]**

Guard: “Hey! I’m not supposed to exist beyond this moment, but I’m saying it anyway: you three aren’t allowed in the castle.”

Scrappy: “How are you ‘appearance only’ while giving a full speech? That tag’s lying harder than a villain in a rubber mask!”

Guard: “The tag system is broken. Also the castle is haunted. Also I quit.”

**[He walks offscreen, somehow remaining “appearance only.”]**

Shaggy: “Even the NPCs are unionizing. Love that for him.”

**[Inside the castle, a podium reads: “COUNT DRACULA (EVENT ONLY) (NO REFUNDS).” Dracula turns, offended.]**

Dracula: “Welcome back. Again. Somehow. I am Count Dracula—*event only.*”

Scooby: “Revent only? So you’re like… seasonal?”

Shaggy: “Like, what are you, a haunted pop-up store?”

Dracula: “I will turn your friend into a werewolf and—”

Shaggy: “Been there, howled that. Also why does your tag have ‘NO REFUNDS’?”

Dracula: “Because you keep returning.”

**[A racer strides up with a sash: “Wolfgang (one note).”]**

Wolfgang: “Grr.”

Scrappy: “That’s the whole note, huh? Budget cuts hit dialogue too?”

Wolfgang: “Grr… *with nuance.*”

**[Frankenstein stomps over wearing a placard: “Frankenstein (skin only) (still deeply moisturized).”]**

Frankenstein: “I am only skin. Please do not ask about organs.”

Shaggy: “Like, I wasn’t going to, but now I’m *curious.*”

Scooby: “Rhis is rvery un-educational!”

**[A witch hovers in with a folder labeled: “Disclaimer (for illustrative purposes only).”]**

Disclaimer: “As a disclaimer, I would like to clarify that all curses are metaphorical.”

Scrappy: “If the curse is metaphorical, why do I keep getting punched by metaphorical furniture?”

Disclaimer: “That is also illustrative.”

**[Dracula snaps his fingers; Shaggy’s shirt rips slightly—half-transform starting.]**

Shaggy: “Like, can you not? I’m mid-wardrobe cycle!”

Dracula: “You will be restored only if you win Monster of the Year.”

Scooby: “Ragain??”

Scrappy: “Deja vu so strong I can taste it. And it tastes like ‘terms and conditions.’”

**[A clipboard floats by itself reading: “Race Rules (external only) (still yelling).”]**

Race Rules: “NO CHEATING! NO HEROICS! NO IMPROVISED PHYSICS!”

Shaggy: “Like, ‘external only’ but it’s yelling at us? That’s basically every app notification.”

Scooby: “Rturn off push notifications!”

**[They storm back outside; the van decal now reads: “Shaggy Van (correct-ish).”]**

Shaggy: “Oh NOW you wanna act normal.”

Scrappy: “Too late! I’ve already emotionally keyed the ‘Vanne’ incident into my soul.”

Scooby: “Rwe’re gonna win this race… and then file a complaint with whoever keeps renaming the car!”

**[Dracula, from the doorway:]**

Dracula: “Complaints are… (read only).”

Shaggy: “Like, of course they are.”

**[Cue chase-music sting as the Monster Road Race revs up again—same track, different tags.]**

**THE END (loop only)**

Before we start—stop calling it Shaggy’s van from *13 Ghosts*. That was a bit we did for the rebrand episodes. For this one, they’re back in the Mystery Machine, and they’re gonna complain about how it keeps getting swapped every time reality hiccups.

Shaggy: Like, THANK you—because every other week it’s “Shaggy Van,” “Shaggy Wan,” “Shaggy Wayne,” and whatever “Bonsage” was.

Scooby: Ryeah! It’s not always the *13 Ghosts* van—sometimes it’s the Mystery Machine! Rclassic! Rgreen! Rlegally questionable upholstery!

Scrappy: And if anybody says “from The 13 Ghosts” again, I’m gonna bite the script supervisor! We’ve been vehicle-swapped like six times!

Shaggy: So yeah—Mystery Machine today. Until the universe clicks “rename all” again.
